---
title: "Analysis of MP's expenses"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-06"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
```

```{r load_data}
time_series <- readRDS(here("outputs", "time_series.RDS"))
```

```{r key_info}
election_years <- c(2015, 2017, 2019, 2024)
```

```{r spending_over_time_individual}
time_series |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year_start,
             y = derived_total)) +
  geom_jitter() +
  geom_boxplot(aes(group = year_start)) +
  geom_smooth()

```

```{r spending_over_time_summaries}
# Summarise total spend by year
spend_by_year <- time_series |>
  group_by(year_start) |>
  summarise(
    total_spend = sum(derived_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    average_spend = mean(derived_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_mps = n_distinct(p_id)
  ) |>
  arrange(year_start)

# Plot total spend over time
ggplot(spend_by_year, aes(x = year_start, y = total_spend)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Total MP Expenses by Financial Year",
    x = "Financial Year",
    y = "", 
    subtitle = "Total Spend (£)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r spend-by-category}
spend_cols <- c("office_spend",
                "staffing_spend",
                "wind_up_spend",
                "accommodation_spend",
                "travel_and_subsistence_spend",
                "other_costs_spend",
                "start_up_spend"
                )

spend_by_category <- time_series |>
  select(p_id, start_year, all_of(spend_cols)) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(spend_cols),
    names_to = "category",
    values_to = "spend"
  ) |>
  group_by(start_year, category) |>
  summarise(mean_spend = mean(spend, na.rm = TRUE),
            total_spend = sum(spend, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

spend_by_category |>
  ggplot(aes(x = start_year, y = total_spend, colour = category)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = election_years,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "red",
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  # facet_wrap(~ category, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Total MP Spend (£) by Category and Financial Year",
    subtitle = "Dashed lines indicate UK General Elections",
    x = "Financial Year",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r staffing_spend_vs_budget}
staffing_compare <- time_series |>
  group_by(start_year) |>
  summarise(
    mean_budget = mean(staffing_budget, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_spend  = mean(staffing_spend, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean_budget, mean_spend),
    names_to = "type",
    values_to = "value"
  ) |>
  mutate(type = recode(type,
    "mean_budget" = "Budget",
    "mean_spend"  = "Spend"
  ))

staffing_compare |>
  ggplot(aes(x = start_year, y = value, colour = type)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = election_years,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "red",
    alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Budget" = "steelblue", "Spend" = "darkorange")) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean MP Staffing Budget vs Spend by Financial Year",
    subtitle = "Dashed lines indicate UK General Elections",
    x = "Financial Year",
    y = "Mean (£)",
    colour = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
